codeReview.summary: The worker updated README.md (commit aec905c) to replace all references to Copilot's old local token discovery mechanism with the new GitHub Device Code Flow. The config.example.yaml was already correctly updated by a prior worker (commit 800db77). The changes are clean, accurate, and consistent with the actual codebase implementation.

issues.description: The Configuration Reference section says 'Optional: OAuth configuration (codex backend only)' but Copilot backends can also use an oauth.client_id override. The dedicated Copilot config section correctly documents this, but the generic reference section's parenthetical is slightly misleading. This pre-existed the worker's changes and is not a regression, but could be updated to '(codex and copilot backends)' for accuracy.

summary: Feature passes review. The config.example.yaml shows a clean Copilot config with type, base_url, and optional oauth.client_id (defaulting to Iv1.b507a08c87ecfe98). The README.md Copilot section was fully rewritten to document the Device Code Flow with step-by-step instructions. All old references to GH_TOKEN, gh CLI, hosts.yml, and local token discovery have been removed from both files. The handoff notes that config.example.yaml was already updated by a prior worker, and the worker correctly focused only on README.md updates. Build, vet, and tests all passed. One minor non-blocking issue: the generic Configuration Reference section labels OAuth as 'codex backend only' which is slightly inaccurate since Copilot can also accept an oauth.client_id override, but this pre-existed the feature and is not a regression.
